mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and compress equity valuations through higher discount rates, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean direction in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and retail sales indicate a synchronized contraction in residential and consumer demand, which reduces corporate revenue forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in HOUST and RSAFS fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.04, or positive returns occur in >50% of co-decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that outpaces demand destruction, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during tightening periods yield negative 5-day returns in >55% of cases, or the mechanism shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A widening gap between the federal funds rate and the consumer price index indicates real rate compression that improves equity risk premiums, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The real rate spread fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.03, or returns are negative in >60% of high-spread observations.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls sustains aggregate income and corporate earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates negatively with 60-day forward returns, or the signal produces positive returns in <45% of observations over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth rate falling below 1.5% signals severe demand weakness that triggers earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth below the threshold is followed by positive 5-day returns in >55% of cases, or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample validation (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a housing market correction reflects resilient manufacturing demand that supports equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during housing corrections yield negative 20-day returns in >60% of instances, or the regime-specific predictive power vanishes (Rank IC < 0.02).
